title: Finding Slope from Two Points: (2, 1) and (5, 7)

steps.explanation: It does not matter which point you call (x1, y1) — the slope comes out the same either way.
Rise measures the vertical change — how much the line goes up (or down if negative).
Run measures the horizontal change — how far you move to the right.
A slope of 2 means for every 1 unit you move right, the line goes up 2 units.

title: Finding Slope with Negative Coordinates: (-3, 4) and (1, -2)

steps.explanation: Be especially careful with signs when coordinates are negative.
A negative rise means the line goes down from left to right.
Subtracting a negative is the same as adding: 1 - (-3) = 1 + 3.
A slope of -3/2 means for every 2 units right, the line drops 3 units. The line falls from left to right.
